name: Pakistan
summary: Emerged as key mediator in the Iran war. Hosted Islamabad quadrilateral meeting (March 29-30) with Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Egypt FMs. Offered to host direct US-Iran talks 'in coming days.' FM Ishaq Dar publicly confirmed Pakistan relaying messages between US and Iran. Pakistan is the ONLY confirmed intermediary channel both sides acknowledge exists.

signals.content: Pakistan FM Ishaq Dar publicly confirmed 'US-Iran indirect talks are taking place through messages being relayed by Pakistan' — first third-party validation of a communication channel. Turkey and Egypt also helping relay messages. (Sources: NPR, OPB, CNBC)

Pakistan hosted Islamabad quadrilateral meeting — FMs from Saudi Arabia (Prince Faisal bin Farhan), Turkey (Hakan Fidan), Egypt (Badr Abdelatti) met in Islamabad. Turkish FM Fidan sought to 'establish a mechanism aimed at de-escalation.' No joint statement issued. Talks continued March 30. (Sources: Bloomberg, NPR, Al Jazeera, Business Standard, Egyptian Streets)

Pakistan offered to host direct US-Iran talks 'in coming days' — elevating from bilateral message relay to hosting venue for potential face-to-face negotiations. The quad exchanged 'assessments on dire economic repercussions' including supply chains, food security, energy security. Pakistan positioned as neutral mediator — maintains relations with both Iran (shared border, Balochistan) and US (military/intelligence cooperation). (Sources: Bloomberg, NPR, Time, Al Jazeera)

edges.context: Pakistan is the confirmed message relay between US and Iran. Only intermediary both sides acknowledge. Offered to host direct talks.
Pakistan relaying US messages to Iran. Offered to host US-Iran talks. US envoy Witkoff presented 15-point plan via Pakistan.
Saudi FM attended Islamabad quad. Saudi-Pakistan coordination on de-escalation. Both directly affected by Iranian strikes.
Pakistan's mediation directly affects oil prices — the Islamabad quad + hosting offer contributed to Brent's -4.3% pullback on March 30.

trigger_points.condition: Pakistan hosts direct US-Iran face-to-face talks
trigger_points.mechanism: Direct talks would be the biggest de-escalation signal of the war. Oil could drop 10-15% in a session. Rupee rally. Nifty surge. But talks ≠ deal — the gap between positions remains enormous.
